exam.name: Mathematics (5165)
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for prospective secondary mathematics teachers with syllabus-mapped coverage across algebra, functions and calculus, geometry, and statistics and probability—including teaching-scenario items.
exam.hero_headline: Pass Praxis 5165 with secondary math mapped to the official objectives
exam.hero_subheadline: Study Number & Quantity through Statistics & Probability in focused 15-minute topics aligned to the ETS Study Companion.
exam.companion_problem: Most Praxis math prep either drills mechanics without the teaching frame or scatters topics so you cannot see how the exam weights algebra, geometry, and stats.
exam.companion_solution: We translate the official ETS syllabus into a full learning map with topic-sized notes, consistent coverage of instructional scenarios, and retention-focused structure.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring secondary mathematics teacher preparing for selected-response and numeric-entry items, including tasks set in teaching and curriculum contexts. Comfortable with a graphing calculator; benefits from clear domain boundaries and scenario-based practice.

sections.title: Number & Quantity and Algebra
sections.description: Understand the structure of real and complex number systems, apply quantitative reasoning, rewrite algebraic expressions, and solve and graph equations and inequalities in one or more variables.

topics.title: Structure of the Real Number System
topics.description: Structure and properties of the real number system including rational and irrational numbers.
learning_objectives: Identify rational and irrational numbers
Apply commutative, associative, and distributive properties
Solve problems using number theory properties (e.g., primes, factors, multiples)

topics.title: Ratios, Rates, and Percents
topics.description: Use proportional relationships to solve ratio, constant rate, and percent problems.
learning_objectives: Solve ratio and constant rate problems
Calculate percent problems using proportional relationships

topics.title: Radicals and Rational Exponents
topics.description: Properties of radicals, rational exponents, and scientific notation.
learning_objectives: Perform operations involving rational exponents
Rewrite expressions with radicals
Perform calculations using scientific notation

topics.title: Quantitative Reasoning and Units
topics.description: Reason quantitatively using units, dimensions, and scales to solve measurement problems.
learning_objectives: Interpret scales and origins in graphs
Solve measurement and conversion problems
Apply dimensional analysis

topics.title: Complex Number System
topics.description: Structure of complex numbers and their basic operations.
learning_objectives: Perform operations with complex numbers and conjugates
Apply commutative, associative, and distributive properties to complex numbers

topics.title: Algebraic Expressions and Polynomials
topics.description: Write, rearrange, and evaluate algebraic expressions and polynomials.
learning_objectives: Rewrite algebraic expressions in equivalent forms
Factor special polynomials over complex numbers
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide polynomials

topics.title: Creating Equations and Inequalities
topics.description: Create linear and nonlinear equations and inequalities that describe relationships.
learning_objectives: Create equations in one variable
Create equations and inequalities in two variables
Represent constraints by systems of equations and interpret solutions as viable options

topics.title: Solving Linear Equations and Inequalities
topics.description: Techniques for solving linear equations and inequalities in one or more variables.
learning_objectives: Solve linear equations and inequalities in one variable
Graph solutions to linear inequalities in two variables
Calculate and interpret intercepts and slopes in modeling contexts

topics.title: Solving Quadratic Equations
topics.description: Techniques for solving quadratic equations with real and complex solutions.
learning_objectives: Solve quadratic equations by factoring and completing the square
Use the quadratic formula
Determine the nature of roots using discriminant analysis

topics.title: Solving Systems of Equations
topics.description: Solve systems of linear and non-linear equations.
learning_objectives: Solve systems of two linear equations algebraically and graphically
Solve a system of a linear and a quadratic equation

topics.title: Polynomial Functions and Zeros
topics.description: Analyze the relationship between zeros of polynomial functions and their factors.
learning_objectives: Apply the remainder theorem to find factors
Identify zeros of polynomials through factorization
Sketch polynomial graphs using zeros

topics.title: Rational and Radical Equations
topics.description: Solve and justify the reasoning for rational and radical equations, accounting for extraneous solutions.
learning_objectives: Rewrite rational expressions
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational expressions
Solve rational and radical equations and identify extraneous solutions

sections.title: Functions and Calculus
sections.description: Understand function behavior, modeling relationships, limits, continuity, and apply techniques of differentiation and integration.

topics.title: Function Notation and Relations
topics.description: Understand function notation, and determine domains and ranges.
learning_objectives: Determine whether a relation is a function
Evaluate functions and interpret function notation in context
Find the domain and range of a function

topics.title: Analyzing Function Behavior
topics.description: Analyze graphs and tables to interpret key features of functions.
learning_objectives: Interpret increasing/decreasing behavior, extrema, and end-behavior
Sketch graphs from verbal descriptions
Graph linear, quadratic, exponential, polynomial, rational, piecewise, and trigonometric functions

topics.title: Building and Transforming Functions
topics.description: Obtain new functions through compositions, transformations, and inverses.
learning_objectives: Describe graphical transformations given analytical modifications
Find and interpret inverse functions
Analyze domains of functions combined by arithmetic operations and compositions

topics.title: Linear, Quadratic, and Exponential Models
topics.description: Compare properties and models of linear, quadratic, and exponential functions.
learning_objectives: Contrast linear, quadratic, and exponential growth rates
Construct linear and exponential functions from context or data points
Calculate and interpret the average rate of change

topics.title: Arithmetic and Geometric Sequences
topics.description: Translate between recursive and explicit forms of arithmetic and geometric sequences.
learning_objectives: Write explicit and recursive formulas for sequences
Model real-world situations with arithmetic and geometric sequences

topics.title: Logarithmic Functions and Properties
topics.description: Use logarithmic properties to solve exponential and logarithmic equations.
learning_objectives: Apply properties of logarithms
Solve exponential equations using logarithms
Use technology to evaluate logarithms with any base

topics.title: Trigonometric Functions and the Unit Circle
topics.description: Understand the unit circle and values of trigonometric functions.
learning_objectives: Convert between degree and radian measures
Identify reference angles and evaluate trigonometric functions
Explain symmetry and periodicity using the unit circle

topics.title: Modeling Periodic Phenomena
topics.description: Model periodic phenomena using trigonometric functions and identities.
learning_objectives: Model periodic situations specifying amplitude, frequency, and midline
Solve trigonometric equations that arise in modeling contexts
Use inverse trigonometric functions

topics.title: Limits of Functions
topics.description: Understand and calculate limits of functions, including one-sided limits and limits to infinity.
learning_objectives: Solve limit problems using properties of limits
Determine one-sided limits
Compute limits to infinity and identify limits that do not exist

topics.title: Continuity and Differentiability
topics.description: Understand continuity at a point and its relationship with differentiability.
learning_objectives: Apply the three conditions defining continuity at a point
Provide examples of continuous functions that are not differentiable
Understand the relationship between continuity and differentiability

topics.title: The Derivative
topics.description: Understand the derivative as a limit, the slope of a tangent line, and a rate of change.
learning_objectives: Relate the sequence of secant slopes to the slope of a tangent line
Calculate derivatives using the limit definition
Interpret derivatives as rates of change in modeling contexts

topics.title: Standard Differentiation Techniques
topics.description: Calculate derivatives using the standard differentiation rules.
learning_objectives: Calculate derivatives using power, product, and quotient rules
Apply the chain rule
Calculate derivatives of exponential, logarithmic, and trigonometric functions

topics.title: Analyzing Functions with Derivatives
topics.description: Use the first and second derivatives to analyze function behavior.
learning_objectives: Find extreme values and intervals of increasing/decreasing
Determine concavity and find points of inflection
Analyze graphs using first and second derivatives

topics.title: Integration and Applications
topics.description: Standard integration techniques, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, and their applications.
learning_objectives: Compute definite and indefinite integrals
Apply the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
Use integration to compute areas between curves
Relate position, velocity, and acceleration functions using calculus

sections.title: Geometry
sections.description: Apply properties of geometric figures, perform transformations, prove theorems, use trigonometry, and solve problems involving area and volume.

topics.title: Lines and Angles
topics.description: Properties of parallel, perpendicular, and intersecting lines and angle relationships.
learning_objectives: Solve problems involving parallel, perpendicular, and intersecting lines
Apply supplementary, vertical, and alternate interior angle relationships

topics.title: Triangles and Polygons
topics.description: Properties and theorems related to triangles, quadrilaterals, and other polygons.
learning_objectives: Apply the triangle inequality theorem
Solve problems with special right, equilateral, and isosceles triangles
Identify geometric properties of quadrilaterals such as parallelograms and rectangles

topics.title: Transformations in the Plane
topics.description: Use rigid motions and dilations to transform figures and describe symmetry.
learning_objectives: Apply translations, rotations, and reflections
Apply properties of dilations (preserving angle measure but not distance)
Identify sequences of transformations mapping a preimage onto an image

topics.title: Congruence and Similarity
topics.description: Determine and prove congruence and similarity using theorems and rigid motions.
learning_objectives: Determine congruence using ASA, SAS, and SSS theorems
Determine similarity using the AA criterion
Determine congruence or similarity by mapping figures using sequences of transformations

topics.title: Geometric Proofs
topics.description: Prove geometric theorems about lines, angles, triangles, and parallelograms.
learning_objectives: Prove theorems about lines and angles
Prove theorems involving triangles, medians, and the Pythagorean theorem
Evaluate the validity of geometric proofs, including direct proofs and counterexamples

topics.title: Trigonometry in Triangles
topics.description: Apply trigonometric ratios, the Law of Sines, and the Law of Cosines.
learning_objectives: Use the relationship between sine and cosine of complementary angles
Solve side lengths and angles in right triangles using trigonometric ratios
Apply the Law of Sines and the Law of Cosines to find unknown measurements in non-right triangles

topics.title: Circle Properties and Theorems
topics.description: Theorems about circles, arcs, angles, and sectors.
learning_objectives: Solve problems involving circumference and area of a circle
Solve problems involving lengths of arcs and areas of sectors
Solve problems involving inscribed and central angles, chords, secants, tangents, and radii

topics.title: Coordinate Geometry
topics.description: Use the coordinate plane to describe shapes and solve geometric problems.
learning_objectives: Determine distance and midpoints in the coordinate plane
Find the point that partitions a directed line segment in a given ratio
Determine the equation of a circle given its center and radius

topics.title: Perimeter, Area, and Volume
topics.description: Solve problems involving perimeter, area, surface area, and volume of 2D and 3D objects.
learning_objectives: Calculate perimeter and area of composed polygons
Calculate surface area and volume of prisms, pyramids, cylinders, cones, and spheres
Calculate changes in area or volume as the dimensions of a polygon or solid change

topics.title: Cross Sections and 3D Objects
topics.description: Visualize and identify two-dimensional cross-sections and rotations of objects.
learning_objectives: Identify the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of three-dimensional objects
Identify three-dimensional objects generated by the rotation of two-dimensional objects
Use two-dimensional nets to visualize and solve problems involving three-dimensional figures

sections.title: Statistics & Probability
sections.description: Summarize and interpret single and bivariate data, understand study design, construct probability models, and work with normal distributions.

topics.title: Sampling and Study Design
topics.description: Make inferences and justify conclusions from samples, experiments, and observational studies.
learning_objectives: Identify differences among sample surveys, experiments, and observational studies
Explain how randomization relates to study design
Use data from a sample survey to estimate a population mean or proportion

topics.title: Summarizing Single-Variable Data
topics.description: Summarize, represent, and interpret data collected from a single variable.
learning_objectives: Represent and interpret data using dot plots, histograms, and boxplots
Compute measures of center (mean, median) and spread (standard deviation, interquartile range)
Interpret differences in shape, center, and spread accounting for possible outliers

topics.title: Bivariate Data and Scatterplots
topics.description: Represent and interpret categorical and quantitative data for two variables.
learning_objectives: Summarize and interpret categorical data in two-way frequency tables
Identify associations and trends in scatterplots
Calculate conditional relative frequencies

topics.title: Linear Regression and Correlation
topics.description: Create and interpret linear regression models and correlation coefficients.
learning_objectives: Fit a linear regression function to data
Interpret the slope and intercept of a regression line in context
Distinguish between correlation and causation
Assess the fit of a function by analyzing residuals

topics.title: Simple and Compound Probability
topics.description: Compute probabilities of simple, compound, and conditional events.
learning_objectives: Describe events as subsets of a sample space (unions, intersections, complements)
Identify and apply the concepts of conditional probability and independence
Construct and interpret two-way frequency tables to approximate conditional probabilities

topics.title: Probability Rules and Counting
topics.description: Apply the addition and multiplication rules alongside combinatorics to solve problems.
learning_objectives: Apply the addition rule and general multiplication rule
Use the fundamental counting principle to find probabilities of independent trials
Use permutations and combinations to solve counting problems

topics.title: Expected Values and Decisions
topics.description: Interpret probability distributions and expected values to make decisions.
learning_objectives: Interpret a probability distribution for a random variable
Find the expected value of a random variable
Weigh possible outcomes of a decision by assigning probabilities and finding expected values

topics.title: Normal Distributions
topics.description: Understand and apply the properties of normal distributions.
learning_objectives: Identify whether data sets are normally distributed based on shape
Use the mean and standard deviation of a normal distribution to interpret population percentages
Estimate and interpret areas under the normal curve using the empirical rule
